fields.title: agent: track parent-child relationships
fields.description: Add parentAgentId tracking so the manager knows which agent spawned which. This is the foundation for ACL and cascading teardown.nnerg-effect reference: AgentActor stores parentAgentId as Option<AgentId>. SpawnOptions accepts parentAgentId.
fields.design: Add optional parentId field to SpawnOptions in services.ts. Store a second Ref<HashMap<AgentId, AgentId>> (childToParent map) in AgentManagerLive. On spawn, record the mapping. On shutdown, remove it. Add parentId to AgentInfo so list can expose it. Thread parentSessionId (which is the spawning agent's session) as parentId from AgentControl.spawn through to manager.spawn.
fields.acceptance_criteria: After spawning agent B from agent A, the manager can answer 'who is B's parent?' (A). AgentInfo includes the parent id. Removing an agent cleans up its parent tracking entry.
fields.notes: Addressed review follow-ups for parent-child plumbing: added parentAgentId to ControlSpawnOptions; threaded opts.parentAgentId through control.spawn -> manager.spawn; added parentAgentId to AgentToolContext and passed it in tool spawn; set main-session context parentAgentId undefined in agent/index.ts; set worker agent tool context parentAgentId=agentId in agent/worker.ts so worker-spawned children inherit parent agent id. Ran npm run gate in extensions/tau (typecheck/lint/test pass; existing lint warnings unchanged).
fields.close_reason: Implemented and reviewed. parentAgentId plumbed through entire stack. Also fixed pre-existing depthMap key mismatch bug.
